title: Controversial Take
content: Stop wasting money on ads. Organic content beats paid every single time if you know what you're doing. Here's why 99% of marketers get this backwards...
notes: Use this when your audience is frustrated with ad costs

content: Your 'dream clients' don't exist. And chasing them is killing your business. The sooner you accept this, the faster you'll scale.

title: Quick List Hook
content:
5 affiliate mistakes that cost me $10K last month:

1. Promoting products I didn't use
2. Ignoring email sequences
3. No tracking pixels
4. Generic content
5. Not building trust first

Here's what I changed...
notes: Lists perform 3x better than regular posts

content:

**AGITATE:** What happens when you get sick? Or want a vacation? Your income stops. Your clients wait. You burn out.

**SOLUTION:** Group coaching + automated systems = predictable income without the 1:1 grind. DM me 'SCALE' to learn how.

content:
I totally understand! Investment is a big decision.

Quick question: If this could save you 6 months of trial and error, would that be worth it?

Most clients make back their investment in the first 30 days. But if you're not ready, no pressure. Want me to check back in a month?

I appreciate you being cautious! There's a lot of noise out there.

Here's what I can offer: Check my testimonials, DM 3 of my current clients, and review the full curriculum before you decide.

I'm not here to convince you—just to show you what's possible. Fair?

Totally get it—timing matters!

Can I ask: what needs to happen first before you'd be ready?

[Wait for response]

Got it. How about this: I'll save your spot for 2 weeks. If things align, great. If not, no worries. Does that work?

title: Course Graveyard
content:
Your $97 course isn't selling because your content is boring. Let's be real.

People don't need more information. They need transformation they can see in 7 days.

Stop teaching. Start showing.
notes: Hits course creators where it hurts

title: Engagement Theater
content:
You're not building a community. You're running an engagement farm.

Likes don't pay bills. DMs do.

Time to stop performing and start selling.

title: Link Dumping
content:
Dropping affiliate links in stories isn't a strategy. It's spam.

Your audience can smell desperation through the screen.

Here's what actually converts...

title: Template Trap
content:
Selling templates is a race to the bottom.

Everyone has templates now. Canva has templates. AI makes templates.

You need a business model, not another PDF.

title: Guru Complex
content:
Most coaches are just 3 steps ahead pretending to be 300.

The truth? That's actually fine.

You don't need to be perfect. You need to be useful.

title: Content Mistakes
content:
5 reasons your content gets views but zero sales:

1. You entertain, never sell
2. No clear next step
3. Talking to everyone = talking to no one
4. You're solving fake problems
5. Zero urgency

Pick one. Fix it today.

title: Pricing Errors
content:
3 pricing mistakes keeping you broke:

• Charging what YOU can afford
• Comparing yourself to beginners
• Itemizing your time instead of selling outcomes

Your price should make you slightly uncomfortable. If it doesn't, you're undercharging.

title: Trust Killers
content:
7 things that make you look like a scammer:

1. Fake urgency ("Only 2 spots left!" for 6 months)
2. Screenshots with no context
3. Promising results you can't control
4. Income claims with zero proof
5. Following up 47 times
6. "Hey babe" DMs
7. Over-selling in every post

Stop it.

title: Launch Failures
content:
Why your last launch flopped:

→ You didn't pre-sell
→ You built in silence
→ You launched to cold traffic
→ Your offer was confusing
→ You gave up after 48 hours

Launches aren't events. They're processes.

title: SEO Myths
content:
4 Etsy SEO lies you need to unlearn:

1. More tags = more traffic (wrong)
2. Copying top sellers works (it doesn't)
3. SEO is a one-time setup (lol no)
4. Keywords matter more than conversions (backwards)

SEO gets them there. Your listing converts them.

title: Revenue Shift
content:
6 months ago: 10K followers, $800/month.

Today: 11K followers, $14K/month.

The difference? I stopped creating content for engagement and started creating content for conversion.

Same audience. Different intention.

title: Client Quality
content:
January: 12 clients at $500 each. Exhausted.

June: 4 clients at $3K each. Energized.

The work didn't get easier. My boundaries got clearer.

Less clients. Better clients. Same revenue. More life.

title: Trust Metrics
content:
Spent 3 months building trust. Zero promotions.

Then dropped ONE affiliate link.

$4,200 in 48 hours.

The sale is easy when the relationship is solid. Stop rushing it.

title: Offer Pivot
content:
Sold 9 copies of my course in 4 months. Nightmare.

Split it into a $47 mini-course. Sold 89 copies in 6 weeks.

Same content. Smaller commitment. Bigger results.

Your offer might not be bad. It might just be too big.

title: System Over Hustle
content:
Used to work 60 hours a week. Made $4K/month.

Now I work 20 hours a week. Make $11K/month.

What changed? I built systems instead of grinding harder.

More hours doesn't equal more money. Better systems do.

title: Been Burned Response
content:
Look, I get it. You've been burned by courses before.

Most are fluff. This isn't.

Check the results. Read the testimonials. DM three people who've gone through it.

If you still think it's BS after that, fair. But do your homework first.

title: Trust Deficit
content:
Honestly? You shouldn't trust me yet.

Trust is earned. So here's the deal: hop on a 15-min call. I'll give you one strategy you can use today. Free.

No pitch. Just value. Then you decide.

title: Screenshot Skepticism
content:
Fair question. Anyone can fake screenshots.

Here's what I can do: I'll screen-share my actual dashboard. Live. Unedited.

You tell me when works. I'll show you the real numbers. No smoke and mirrors.

title: Results Doubt
content:
The truth is, not everyone gets the same results. And I'm not going to lie about that.

What I CAN promise: you'll get the exact system that worked for 80+ people.

Results depend on execution. I give you the map. You walk the path. Sound fair?

title: Refund Policy Check
content:
60-day money-back guarantee. Full refund. No questions.

You try it. If it sucks, you get your money back.

I'm confident enough in this to put my money where my mouth is. Are you confident enough to try?

title: ROI Breakdown
content:
Here's the math:

$1,997 investment.

If you land ONE client at $2K, you're profitable.

Most students land 3-5 clients in the first 60 days. So you're not spending $2K. You're making $4K-$10K.

Still expensive?

title: Compare to Courses
content:
You've spent what, $1,500 on courses in the last year?

How much have you made from them? Be honest.

This costs the same as 3 courses. But you get direct access to ME. Not a Facebook group. Not recorded videos. Actual coaching.

See the difference?

title: Monthly Break-Even
content:
Break it down monthly: $297/month for 6 months.

That's $10/day.

You're telling me you can't make an extra $10/day with a proven affiliate system? Because if that's true, we have bigger problems.

title: DIY Cost
content:
Cool. DIY it.

But real talk—how much is your time worth? $50/hour? $100?

You'll spend 40+ hours figuring this out. That's $2K-$4K in lost time.

Or you could pay $497 and have it done in 2 weeks. Your call.

title: Profit Per Product
content:
You make what, $15 profit per product?

You need to sell 100 more units to cover this.

If I show you how to get 100 extra sales in 60 days, is it worth it? Because that's literally Module 3.

title: Perfect Time Myth
content:
There's never a perfect time. You know that.

You're always going to be busy. Always going to have something else going on.

The question is: do you want to be busy and broke, or busy and profitable?

title: Readiness Trap
content:
You'll never feel ready. That's not how this works.

Every successful person I know started before they were ready. That's the point.

Ready is a myth. Start is a decision.

title: Audience Size Excuse
content:
You don't need 10K followers to make money.

I've seen people with 800 followers make $5K/month. Because they had the right offer and the right strategy.

You're using audience size as an excuse. Stop it.

title: Product First Logic
content:
So you're going to build the product, THEN learn how to sell it?

That's backwards.

Learn the sales process first. Then build what people actually want. Not the other way around.

title: Next Quarter Delay
content:
Cool. So what's different in Q2?

Because if nothing changes, you'll be in the same spot you are now.

Waiting doesn't fix problems. Action does. And you can start today.

title: Fake Waitlists
content:
Your 'exclusive waitlist' isn't exclusive.

It's a glorified email list you never email.

People don't want to wait. They want to buy. Let them.

title: Algorithm Excuse
content:
Blaming the algorithm is lazy.

Your content isn't suppressed. It's just not interesting.

The algorithm rewards engagement. Make something engaging.

title: Handmade Premium
content:
Charging $200 for a candle because it's 'handmade' doesn't work anymore.

Handmade isn't a value proposition. It's table stakes.

What problem does your candle solve? That's your value.

title: Passive Income Lie
content:
Passive income isn't passive.

You grind for 6 months building systems. THEN it gets easier.

Anyone selling you 'make money while you sleep' on Day 1 is lying.

title: Niching Down Fear
content:
You're scared to niche down because you think you'll lose opportunities.

The truth? You're already losing them by being generic.

Riches are in niches. Stop fighting it.

title: Content Pillars
content:
4 types of content you need every week:

1. Education (teach something)
2. Inspiration (show a win)
3. Connection (share something real)
4. Conversion (ask for the sale)

Most people do #1 and #3. Then wonder why they're broke.

title: Sales Call Mistakes
content:
5 reasons your sales calls don't convert:

• You're pitching, not listening
• You answer objections before they come up
• You talk about features, not outcomes
• You don't ask for the sale
• You let them 'think about it' without a follow-up plan

Fix one. Watch your close rate jump.

title: Email Sins
content:
3 email mistakes killing your affiliate sales:

1. Selling in every email (you're not building trust)
2. No story (facts tell, stories sell)
3. Weak CTAs (be specific about what you want)

Your list is gold. Stop treating it like a billboard.

title: Pricing Psychology
content:
How to price your digital product:

→ $27-47: Impulse buy, high volume
→ $97-197: Serious buyers, decent support needed
→ $297-497: Premium positioning, must include support
→ $997+: Transformation promise, white-glove experience

Pick your lane. Deliver accordingly.

title: Listing Optimization
content:
6 things to fix in your Etsy listings today:

1. First photo (needs to stop the scroll)
2. Title (front-load your main keyword)
3. Price (are you the cheapest or the best?)
4. Description (benefits first, features second)
5. Reviews (respond to every single one)
6. Shipping (free shipping wins)

Start with #1. It's 80% of your problem.

title: Consistency Payoff
content:
Posted every day for 90 days. Zero sales.

Quit? No.

Kept going.

Day 112: First sale.
Day 140: $3K month.
Day 180: $8K month.

Consistency doesn't pay off immediately. But it always pays off eventually.

title: Boundaries Win
content:
Used to answer DMs at 11pm. Took calls on Sundays. No boundaries.

Result? Burnout and resentment.

Now: Office hours only. No weekends. Clear expectations.

Result? Better clients. Better work. Better life.

Boundaries don't lose you clients. They attract better ones.

title: Niche Specificity
content:
Was promoting everything. Fitness. Finance. Software.

Made $600 in 5 months.

Picked ONE niche. Went all in.

Made $4,100 in 6 weeks.

General = invisible. Specific = profitable.

title: Launch Strategy Shift
content:
First launch: Big hype. Huge promises. 3 sales.

Second launch: Pre-sold to email list. Launched with 40 buyers already locked in.

Same product. Different strategy. 13x results.

Stop launching to strangers.

title: Product Photography
content:
Spent $0 on my product photos. Used my phone. Harsh lighting. Terrible backgrounds.

Sales: 2-3/week.

Invested $150 in a ring light and backdrop.

Sales: 15-20/week.

Presentation matters more than you think.

title: Course Graveyard Objection
content:
The truth is, most courses don't work. Not because they're scams, but because people don't finish them.

This is different. Weekly accountability. Direct access. You're not buying videos. You're hiring a guide.

Still skeptical? Ask to see the curriculum. I'll show you everything.

title: Proof of Concept
content:
Look, I get it. You need proof.

Here's what I'll do: I'll send you 10 client case studies. Real names. Real results. Real screenshots.

If you don't see at least 3 people who started where you are, I'll personally Venmo you $50 for wasting your time.

title: Income Claims Response
content:
Honestly? I don't do income claims. Because your results depend on YOUR work.

What I CAN promise: proven systems, detailed walkthroughs, and support when you get stuck.

I can't make you successful. But I can give you the exact roadmap that worked for 60+ people. Deal?

title: Too Good Check
content:
If it sounds too good to be true, it probably is. I agree.

So let me be clear: this isn't a magic button. It's a system that requires work.

You'll spend 10-15 hours/week for 6 weeks. If you're not willing to do that, save your money. This isn't for you.

title: Commitment Fear
content:
Here's the deal: I don't want your money if you're not committed.

Seriously. I'd rather work with 5 serious people than 50 half-in people.

So before you buy, ask yourself: Am I ready to actually do this? If the answer is no, wait.

title: Ad Spend Comparison
content:
You'll spend $2K on ads that might not work.

But you won't spend $1,497 on the strategy that tells you WHICH ads to run?

That's backwards. Fix the strategy first. Then spend on ads. Not the other way around.

title: Hourly Rate Math
content:
My hourly rate is $500.

This program includes 12 hours of direct coaching. That's $6K in value.

You're paying $2,997. So you're getting 50% off my time, plus the entire system, plus lifetime access.

Still think it's expensive?

title: Free Content Argument
content:
You could piece together free content for 6 months. Totally valid.

Or you could pay $497 and have everything organized, step-by-step, with support.

What's your time worth? Because 6 months of your life costs way more than $497.

title: Payment Plan Option
content:
Can't do $997 upfront? I get it.

Here's the plan: 3 payments of $397.

You start today. You make progress. And by payment 2, you'll have made enough to cover payment 3.

Sound better?

title: Per-Sale Justification
content:
You make $20 profit per sale, right?

This costs $497. So you need 25 sales to break even.

If I can show you how to get 25 extra sales in the next 60 days, is it worth it?

Because that's literally what Module 2 does.

title: Bandwidth Lie
content:
You don't have bandwidth? Or you don't have priorities straight?

Because I guarantee you're spending 10 hours/week scrolling, watching Netflix, or doing busy work.

This takes 5 hours/week. You have the time. You just haven't decided it's important yet.

title: Building in Public First
content:
You want to build an audience before you learn how to monetize?

That's like learning to drive AFTER buying a car.

You build the skillset and the audience at the same time. Not one then the other.

title: Election Year Excuse
content:
The economy's always doing something. Elections. Recessions. Inflation.

Successful people build regardless. Because there's always someone buying.

You can wait for perfect conditions. Or you can build now and win later. Your call.

title: Other Projects Excuse
content:
So you're juggling 3 projects and none of them are making money?

Maybe the problem isn't bandwidth. Maybe it's focus.

Pick one thing. Do it right. Then move to the next. Stop spreading yourself thin.

title: After I Launch Response
content:
You're going to launch... without a proven system?

That's gambling, not strategy.

What if you learned the system DURING your launch? You'd actually know what you're doing. Novel concept, right?

title: Group Coaching Trap
content:
Group coaching is just a webinar with a chatroom.

You're not getting personalized help. You're getting general advice that applies to 10% of the group.

Want real results? Get real support. Not group theory sessions.

title: Follower Obsession
content:
Stop obsessing over follower count.

10K ghost followers won't pay your rent. 500 engaged buyers will.

Vanity metrics are killing your business. Focus on money metrics instead.

title: Underpricing Epidemic
content:
Charging $12 for something that took you 3 hours to make is self-sabotage.

You're not competing on price. You're competing on value.

Raise your prices or quit. Those are your options.

title: Inbox Bombing
content:
Sending 'Hey!' DMs to strangers isn't networking. It's spam.

People can smell copy-paste from a mile away.

Build real relationships or don't DM at all. There's no middle ground.

title: Testimonial Fabrication
content:
If your testimonials are all screenshots with first names only, I don't believe you.

Real testimonials have faces. Full names. Verifiable results.

Stop hiding behind anonymity and show receipts.

title: DM Sequence
content:
The 3-message DM sequence that converts:

1. Compliment something specific (not generic)
2. Ask a question about THEIR business
3. Offer value with zero ask

Then wait. If they're interested, they'll ask. If not, move on.

title: Onboarding Mistakes
content:
3 ways you're losing clients after they pay:

• No clear onboarding process
• Waiting too long for the first session
• Not setting expectations upfront

The sale doesn't end at payment. It starts there.

title: Link Placement
content:
Where to put your affiliate links:

✓ Email (highest conversions)
✓ YouTube descriptions (second best)
✓ Blog posts (SEO long game)
✗ Instagram bio (lowest ROI)
✗ Random stories (desperation vibes)

Put links where people are ready to buy. Not where they're scrolling.

title: Product Validation
content:
How to validate your product before building it:

1. Pre-sell to your email list
2. Run a 3-day challenge
3. Offer beta access at a discount
4. Create a waitlist with deposits

If you can't get 10 people to say yes, don't build it.

title: Review Strategy
content:
How to get more 5-star reviews:

→ Include a thank-you card with a gentle ask
→ Follow up 7 days after delivery
→ Make it stupid easy (direct link)
→ Respond to every review (good or bad)
→ Offer a small bonus for leaving one

Reviews aren't luck. They're systems.

title: Offer Clarity
content:
Had 5 different offers. Confused audience. Made $2K/month.

Killed 4 offers. Focused on ONE. Made $9K/month.

Clarity converts. Confusion kills. Pick one thing and go all in.

title: Niche Pivot
content:
Coached 'entrepreneurs' for 2 years. Struggled to hit $5K months.

Niched down to 'female coaches selling digital products.' Hit $12K in 6 weeks.

Same skills. Different positioning. Massive difference.

title: Content Strategy
content:
Posted random tips for 4 months. Made $300.

Switched to case studies and results. Made $6K in 8 weeks.

People don't buy tips. They buy proof. Show them what's possible.

title: Support Model
content:
Sold a $27 course with zero support. Refund rate: 35%.

Added a private Slack channel and weekly Q&A. Refund rate: 4%.

Support isn't extra. It's essential. People pay for access, not PDFs.

title: Seasonal Strategy
content:
Relied on Q4 for 70% of my revenue. Rest of the year was a struggle.

Built evergreen products that sell year-round. Now every quarter is profitable.

Seasonal income is a trap. Build for consistency, not spikes.

title: Community Proof
content:
Look, I could tell you it works. But that's what everyone says.

Here's what I'll do: I'll add you to our private community for 48 hours. Free.

See how active it is. Ask the members yourself. Then decide if it's legit.

title: Credentials Question
content:
Honestly? I don't have a fancy degree. I don't have a million followers.

What I DO have: 4 years of real results. 80+ clients who've hit their goals. Systems that work.

You want a guru or you want results? Because I'm the second one.

title: Sustainability Question
content:
Fair question. A lot of affiliate strategies are short-term hacks.

This isn't that. We're building audiences, not gaming algorithms.

This worked in 2019. It works now. It'll work in 2027. Because it's based on trust, not tricks.

title: Delivery Concern
content:
The truth? Some people buy and ghost. That's on them, not me.

But if you show up, implement, and ask questions, you'll get everything you need.

I'm not going anywhere. I answer DMs. I show up to calls. You'll have access to me. Period.

title: Comparison Shopping
content:
You're comparing me to 5 other coaches right now. I get it.

Here's the difference: I'm not selling you a course. I'm offering partnership.

You get my number. My Voxer. My brain. That's not scalable. That's why I only take 10 people per quarter.
